topic: Advanced catalogue strategy — what separates good from great
hook: Month 1 you learned what sync is.
cta:
No brief gets fulfilled without these:
→ WAV masters at 24-bit / 48kHz minimum
→ ISRC registered
→ Stems labelled by instrument group (not by track number)
→ BPM and key confirmed
→ Mood and instrumentation tags completed

slides.body: Month 3 question: what separates a catalogue that gets briefs from one that doesn't? ↓

A supervisor doesn't need 400 tracks.
They need 40 tracks that are deeply documented, consistently available, instantly deliverable.

Depth means:
→ Every track has a complete metadata record
→ Every track has stems prepared and labelled
→ Every co-writer credit is documented and signed
→ Rights are confirmed on every piece before any brief arrives Width means: you have a lot of stuff.
Supervisors can't use "a lot of stuff." — — — MOODBOARD YOUR CATALOGUE Great catalogue managers don't think in track titles.
They think in placement types. Build a mood index:
— Upbeat / celebratory (sports, celebration, product launch)
— Tension / build (thriller, drama, documentary)
— Intimate / emotive (healthcare, charity, human interest)
— Epic / cinematic (films, streaming originals, trailers)
— Background / atmospheric (hospitality, retail, ambient brand) Every track should have a primary and secondary placement type.
Supervisors search this way. Speak their language. — — — VERSIONING IS A COMPETITIVE ADVANTAGE One track. Multiple submissions.
Standard: full mix
Instrumental: vocals removed
60-second edit: advertising format
30-second edit: social media / shorter spots
Extended: episodic or documentary use Most composers submit one version.
The composers getting repeat briefs submit five. — — — THE TECHNICAL FLOOR No brief gets fulfilled without these:
→ WAV masters at 24-bit / 48kHz minimum
→ ISRC registered
→ Stems labelled by instrument group (not by track number)
→ BPM and key confirmed
→ Mood and instrumentation tags completed This isn't advanced practice.
It's the baseline. #SyncLicensing #AfricanComposers #CatalogueStrategy #SyncMaster
